title: 10 best herbal decoctions for menopause
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for menopause are a natural way to alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. By steeping dried herbs in hot water, these decoctions can be sipped throughout the day to provide relief from uncomfortable symptoms. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with menopause include peppermint tea to calm digestive issues, red clover tea to balance hormones, and valerian root tea to promote relaxation. These decoctions not only improve physical symptoms but also boost overall well-being, allowing women to regain control over their lives and feel more confident and energetic during this life transition.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with menopause because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the body's nervous system, reducing symptoms of hot flashes and night sweats. The root extract also has a sedative effect on the body, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during this stage of life. Additionally, valerian has natural estrogenic properties that can help alleviate vaginal dryness and itching, further enhancing its benefits for menopausal women seeking relief from these distressing symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxant properties: Valerian helps to reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and anxiety associated with menopause by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as vaginal dryness, itching, and burning by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

GABA-ergic and estrogenic properties: Valerian's ability to stimulate GABA receptors can help regulate mood and reduce anxiety, while its estrogen-like effects may help alleviate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms related to hormonal imbalance.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene alcohol that helps alleviate menopausal symptoms by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Isovaleric acid amide: Isovaleric acid amide is an amide that has sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce the severity of hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings commonly associated with menopause.

Acidic phenolic compounds: Acidic phenolic compounds, such as valerian acid and 4-methoxyvalerenic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and osteoporosis associated with menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which is believed to have a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, helping to alleviate menopause symptoms.
Leaves: Used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats.
Roots: Also used for their estrogenic properties, which may help to balance hormone levels and alleviate menopause symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried roots of valeriana officinalis from a reliable source.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered roots with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes at a low heat to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with menopause because they have a profound impact on hormonal balance. The herb contains compounds that mimic estrogen, helping to alleviate symptoms of hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the severity of vaginal dryness and itching. The decoction also possesses adaptogenic qualities, allowing the body to cope better with stress, which is often exacerbated during menopause. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, St John's Wort decoctions can improve overall quality of life for women going through this transitional phase.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant activity: St John's Wort contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin that have been shown to help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's flavonoids and phenolic acids may help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by hormonal changes during menopause, such as joint pain and vaginal dryness.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of menopausal symptoms like fatigue and insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that helps with menopause by acting as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, thereby reducing hot flashes and anxiety associated with menopause.
Hypericin: A naphthodianthrone with antispasmodic properties, which helps alleviate menopause symptoms such as night sweats and mood swings by reducing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation.

Kaempferol: A flavonoid with antioxidant properties that helps protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause, thereby reducing the risk of chronic diseases and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and xanthones, which are believed to help alleviate menopause symptoms.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils, which may help reduce hot flashes and other menopausal issues.
Roots: Acting as a reservoir for the plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help regulate hormonal balance during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves for each 8-ounce serving of decoction.
Combine the dried herbs with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with menopause because it addresses the hormonal fluctuations that occur during this stage of life. The herb's natural estrogenic properties help to balance and regulate estrogen levels, alleviating symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can be particularly beneficial for women experiencing mood swings and emotional changes during menopause.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and other symptoms associated with menopause.
Hormone-balancing properties: Lemon balm has been traditionally used to balance hormones, which can alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and vaginal dryness during menopause.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Lemon balm's calming and relaxing properties may help reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia often experienced during menopause, promoting better sleep and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
Linalool: A monoterpene that has anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with menopause.
Limonene: A monoterpene with a mild estrogenic effect, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are widely used in decoctions to help alleviate menopausal symptoms due to their rich content of estrogenic compounds and phytoestrogens.
Flowers: The flowers are used in decoctions to reduce hot flashes and night sweats, providing relief from menopausal symptoms due to their estrogenic and anxiolytic properties.

Roots: The roots are used in decoctions to support the endocrine system, particularly during menopause, due to their content of saponins and other phytochemicals that help balance hormonal fluctuations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to release its essential oils and flavor.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the chopped plant material with 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5 to 10 minutes to release its properties.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids to obtain the herbal melissa officinalis decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime decoctions helps with menopause because they offer a natural way to alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The botanicals used in these decoctions, including herbs like ginger, peppermint, and lemongrass, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to regulate hormone imbalances and reduce stress. Additionally, the citrus flavor of lime can stimulate digestion and boost energy levels, further contributing to improved overall health during menopause.
remedy_properties: Phytoestrogens: Herbal lime decoctions contain phytoestrogens, which are plant-derived compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal lime decoctions help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to symptoms such as joint pain, fatigue, and mood swings during menopause.

Amino acids: Herbal lime decoctions contain amino acids like citrulline, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, alleviating hot flashes and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help alleviate menopause symptoms by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce hot flashes and other hormonal changes associated with menopause.
Triterpenoids (Oleane and Ursane): These help with menopause symptoms by exhibiting estrogen-like effects, which can reduce the severity of hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopausal symptoms.

Phenolic acids (Caffeic and Ferulic acids): These help alleviate menopausal symptoms by possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate hot flashes and mood swings associated with menopause.
Flowers: They are used to produce a soothing tea that helps to reduce anxiety and stress, promoting a sense of calm and well-being during menopause.
Barks: They are used for their sedative and anti-anxiety properties to help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of insomnia and restlessness during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Gather tilia x europaea flowers and leaves in equal parts to make a total of 50 grams of dried herb.
Combine the tilia x europaea with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot to start the decoction process.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10 to 15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony decoctions helps with menopause because it targets the underlying hormonal imbalances that occur during this life stage. The herbs in the decoction, such as radix paeoniae alba and radix astragali, help to nourish and balance the body's yin energy, which is often depleted during menopause. This can lead to relief from symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. Additionally, Chinese peony decoctions may also help to regulate menstruation and alleviate vaginal dryness, promoting overall women's health and well-being during this critical period.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory effects: Chinese Peony's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce the severity of hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings associated with menopause.

Hormone-balancing properties: The decoction is believed to tonify (strengthen) the body's Qi and Blood, particularly the Liver and Kidney channels, which helps regulate hormonal imbalances that often occur during menopause.
Sedative and calming effects: Chinese Peony has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness commonly experienced during menopause.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate menopausal symptoms by reducing hot flashes and night sweats through their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Triterpenoids saponins: Triterpenoids saponins in Paeonia lactiflora decoctions have been found to exhibit anti-stress and anti-anxiety effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression often associated with menopause.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Paeonia lactiflora decoctions possess estrogen-like properties, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by regulating the body's estrogen levels and maintaining hormonal balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of paeoniflorin, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety effects.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are thought to help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high concentration of glycosides, which are believed to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal systems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora roots and wash them thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt.
Chop the roots into small pieces weighing about 15-30 grams and dry them in the shade for 2-3 days.
Combine the dried roots with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 30-40 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with menopause because of its ability to soothe and calm the hormonal fluctuations that occur during this transition. The decoction's unique blend of compounds, such as flavonoids and lignans, has a regulatory effect on estrogen levels, reducing symptoms like hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. Additionally, the antioxidant properties in maidenhair tree help to protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate menopausal symptoms. By promoting balance and stability in the body's hormonal system, maidenhair tree decoctions can bring relief and comfort during this transformative stage of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.

Antioxidant properties: The decoctions contain antioxidants that can protect the body against oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to menopausal symptoms such as mood swings and memory loss.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory effects may help improve blood flow to the brain and other areas of the body, potentially alleviating symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and dry skin common during menopause.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps reduce hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause by modulating neurotransmitter activity and improving blood vessel function.

Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and vaginal dryness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Isorhapontigenin: Exhibits estrogen-like properties, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by modulating estrogen receptors and improving estrogen balance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood changes.
Roots: The roots are believed to have a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as mood swings and anxiety.
Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of ginkgolides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menopausal symptoms like hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 liter of boiling water to create decoction.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids from decoction.
Measure 50-100 ml of the decoction and take 2-3 times a day as required by doctor.
Store any remaining decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours to maintain freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American ginseng decoctions helps with menopause because it has been shown to alleviate symptoms of hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The decoction's adaptogenic properties allow it to balance hormones, reducing the intensity and frequency of these uncomfortable symptoms. Additionally, American ginseng has anti-inflammatory effects that may help combat vaginal dryness and itching, making it a natural and effective way for women to manage their menopause symptoms without pharmaceutical intervention.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: American Ginseng helps reduce the severity of hot flashes and night sweats by adapting to the body's needs and promoting balance in the body's response to stress.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in American Ginseng decoctions help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative damage, which are common during menopause.

Hormone-regulating properties: American Ginseng has been shown to modulate estrogen levels and improve hormonal balance, which can alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, insomnia, and vaginal dryness associated with menopause.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate menopausal symptoms by modulating the body's hormonal balance, reducing hot flashes and night sweats.
Coumarins: Specifically, the coumarin scopoletin, helps in regulating estrogen levels and improving sleep quality in menopausal women.
Lignans: These phenolic compounds have been shown to have estrogenic activity, which can help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are most commonly used for menopause decoctions due to their high content of ginsenosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic properties.
Barks: The barks may also be used for menopause decoctions, as they contain ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate menopausal symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves of Panax quinquefolius may be used for menopause decoctions, as they are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including menopause symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 panax quinquefolius roots with a diameter of 1-2 cm and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 1 liter of water in a pot.
Bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 1-2 hours.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with menopause because it has a number of benefits that can alleviate common symptoms. The plant's estrogenic properties help to balance hormonal fluctuations, reducing hot flashes and night sweats. Its anti-inflammatory properties also soothe menstrual cramps and vaginal dryness, while its digestive-aiding compounds ease bloating and constipation. Additionally, fennel's phytoestrogens mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to stabilize mood swings and reduce anxiety. This natural remedy can provide a safe and effective way to manage menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic effects: Fennel's estrogenic properties can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by supporting hormonal balance, as menopause is characterized by a decline in estrogen levels.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities: Fennel's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help reduce the severity of menopausal symptoms such as mood swings, anxiety, and depression by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

Uterine relaxant effects: Fennel's uterine relaxant properties can help soothe and calm menstrual cramps, bloating, and other uterine-related discomforts associated with menopause, allowing women to experience greater comfort and relief.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce hot flashes and night sweats by inhibiting the production of luteinizing hormone, which is often elevated during menopause.
Anethole: A terpene that has estrogen-like effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes, mood swings, and vaginal dryness.

Sesquiterpenes (mainly bisabolol and fenchone): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of menopause symptoms, including hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in estrogenic compounds that help alleviate menopausal symptoms.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are a natural source of estrogenic activity and contain compounds that can ease menopausal discomfort.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils, including fenchone and anethole, which have been used to treat various menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh foeniculum vulgare roots and stems weighing about 1/4 cup or 30 grams for decoction.
Cut the foeniculum vulgare into smaller pieces to release their active compounds for better extraction.
Combine the cut foeniculum vulgare with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the foeniculum vulgare decoction for 5 to 7 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the foeniculum vulgare decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup for serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with menopause because it addresses several symptoms that are common during this stage of life. The herbal remedy is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that help to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to soothe the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and mood swings, while its anti-inflammatory properties may also help with joint pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and joint pain associated with menopause by reducing inflammation in the body.
Estrogen-balancing: Stinging nettle contains phytoestrogens that may help balance estrogen levels, which can reduce symptoms of menopause such as mood swings, vaginal dryness, and insomnia.
Hormone-regulating: Stinging nettle's ability to regulate hormone production can help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopausal symptoms by normalizing hormonal imbalances.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin (IRP): This flavonoid helps alleviate menopausal symptoms by inhibiting the production of inflammatory molecules and promoting the regulation of hormonal balance.

Quercetin: Acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin helps reduce hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopausal symptoms by protecting against oxidative stress and modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.

Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds have been shown to have a beneficial effect on menopausal symptoms by regulating hormonal balance, improving sleep quality, and reducing inflammation, ultimately contributing to a better quality of life during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in isoorientin, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.
Leaves: They contain a high amount of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which may help reduce menopausal symptoms like anxiety and insomnia.
Flowers: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as vaginal dryness and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in the spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and preserve its medicinal properties for up to 6 months.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried urtica dioica with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the plant material and discard the solids.
Store the prepared urtica dioica decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days to maintain its potency and quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with menopause because they possess potent phytoestrogens, which can alleviate hot flashes and night sweats. The flavonoids present in hawthorn also help to soothe nervous tension and anxiety, common symptoms of menopause. Additionally, the antioxidants in hawthorn decoctions may help protect against estrogen-induced oxidative stress, potentially reducing the risk of chronic diseases like osteoporosis and cardiovascular disease. By addressing these symptoms and promoting overall health, hawthorn decoctions can provide relief and support during this transitional phase of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings common during menopause.

Cardiovascular protection: Hawthorn's flavonoids and procyanidins help improve blood vessel function, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, a major concern for postmenopausal women.

Estrogenic activity: Hawthorn contains isoflavones, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and vaginal dryness by mimicking the effects of estrogen in the body.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, help alleviate menopausal symptoms by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby reducing hot flashes and improving cardiovascular health.

Triterpenes: Oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, types of triterpenes found in Crataegus monogyna, have been shown to have estrogen-like effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as vaginal dryness and osteoporosis associated with menopause.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid in Crataegus monogyna decoctions may help regulate hormonal imbalances and improve sleep quality, both of which are common issues during menopause.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are used to alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause due to their estrogenic properties and antioxidant content.
Leaves: Leaves are used to help reduce anxiety, insomnia, and mood swings during menopause, attributed to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Buds are used to soothe menopausal symptoms like hot flashes and night sweats, thanks to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried hawthorn berries, cleaned and stored in airtight containers.
Grind the hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered hawthorn berries with 1000 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then reduce heat to a simmer for an additional 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with menopause because they alleviate symptoms of hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The antispasmodic properties of elderberry help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants and flavonoids present in elderflowers soothe the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Elder decoctions also support hormonal balance by stimulating the liver's detoxification processes, helping to eliminate excess estrogen from the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Elderberry's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hot flashes, night sweats, and mood changes associated with menopause.
Estrogen-regulating: The flavonoids in elderberry may help regulate estrogen levels, alleviating symptoms of menopause such as vaginal dryness, mood swings, and insomnia.
Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by hormonal changes during menopause, including joint pain, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, elder roots help alleviate hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness commonly experienced during menopause.
Barks: The outer layer of elder bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps, mood swings, and other symptoms associated with menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers at the peak of freshness.
Grind the dried herb into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine the powdered herb with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and take 1/2 cup twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with menopause because of its unique combination of phytochemicals that provide relief from symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and sleep disturbances. The herb's flavonoids and volatile oils have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Additionally, lemon verbena's estrogenic properties can help alleviate vaginal dryness and itching, while its antispasmodic properties may ease menstrual cramp discomfort. Overall, this natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective way to manage menopausal symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce symptoms of menopause such as hot flashes, night sweats, and joint pain by mitigating inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: Lemon verbena's anxiolytic properties help alleviate anxiety, mood swings, and irritability commonly experienced during menopause, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.

Hormone-regulating properties: Some studies suggest that lemon verbena may have estrogen-like effects, which could help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with menopause, reducing symptoms such as hot flashes and vaginal dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are believed to be rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Buds: The buds of lemon verbena contain high levels of phenolic compounds, which have been shown to reduce the severity of vaginal dryness and itching during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems in the amount of 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Measure 2 cups of water into a medium-sized pot and bring it to a rolling boil on high heat within 10 minutes.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried verbena officinalis to the boiling water and reduce heat to low, allowing it to simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 5 minutes of straining time.
Allow the verbena officinalis decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed decoctions helps with menopause because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as hot flashes and night sweats. The phytoestrogens present in milkweed bind to estrogen receptors, mimicking the effects of estrogen and providing relief from vaginal dryness and mood swings. Additionally, the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of milkweed may help reduce anxiety and stress associated with menopause. By promoting balance and regulation of hormones, milkweed decoctions can provide a natural and effective approach to managing menopausal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic compounds: Milkweed contains phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats by mimicking the effects of estrogen in the body.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in milkweed decoctions may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause, potentially improving overall health and reducing symptoms like vaginal dryness and mood changes.

Saponins: Milkweed contains saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them potentially helpful in alleviating joint pain, fatigue, and other physical symptoms often experienced during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Seeds: Containing lignans, which have been shown to support bone health and reduce symptoms of osteoporosis common during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried asclepias syriaca flowers, 1 tablespoon of dried st. john's wort, and 4 cups of distilled water.
Combine the asclepias syriaca flowers, st. john's wort, and distilled water in a large pot, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes at a simmer.
Remove the pot from heat and let it sit for 4 hours to allow the mixture to cool and infuse its active ingredients.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the resulting decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 3 days, taking 1/2 cup of it twice daily to alleviate menopausal symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with menopause because they have a profound impact on hormone regulation. The bioactive compounds found in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen and progesterone, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalance. Additionally, licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe menopausal hot flashes and night sweats. By supporting hormone balance and reducing inflammation, licorice decoctions provide natural relief from menopause-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce hot flashes and night sweats by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, making it easier to manage symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, helping to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to menopausal symptoms such as fatigue and mood swings.
Phytoestrogens: Help regulate hormonal balance by binding to estrogen receptors in the body, alleviating symptoms of estrogen deficiency such as vaginal dryness and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats commonly experienced during menopause.
Rhyzomes: Contain antioxidants that help soothe vaginal dryness and reduce inflammation associated with menopausal symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure 10-15 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and add it to a heat-resistant container.
Combine the measured root with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 20-30 minutes, allowing the mixture to steep and extract its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with menopause because it provides relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The decoction's diuretic properties help to alleviate bloating and water retention, a common complaint during menopause. Additionally, dandelion's estrogen-balancing properties may help to ease vaginal dryness and discomfort. By promoting digestion and reducing inflammation, the decoction also helps to alleviate symptoms of joint pain and stiffness. Overall, dandelion decoctions offer a natural and holistic approach to managing the physical and emotional changes associated with menopause.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion's diuretic effect helps to reduce water retention, alleviate bloating and breast tenderness, and support overall menstrual health.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats by soothing the body's response to hormonal fluctuations, while also protecting against cell damage and promoting overall women's health.

Hormone-regulating properties: Dandelion's hormone-balancing effects can help to alleviate symptoms of menopause such as mood swings, fatigue, and sleep disturbances by supporting the body's natural production and regulation of hormones like estrogen.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of menopause such as bloating and irritable bowel syndrome.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as the achene seeds, are rich in calcium and magnesium, which can help to support bone health and reduce the risk of osteoporosis during menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and measure out 10-15 grams per serving.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot and let steep for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container refrigerated at 2-8 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort decoctions helps with menopause because it addresses the symptoms of hormonal imbalance, anxiety, and insomnia that are common during this stage. The herb's properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing hot flashes and night sweats. It also helps to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate mood swings. Additionally, motherwort decoctions promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, making it an effective natural remedy for menopausal women seeking relief from these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Motherwort decoctions have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats.

Hormone Regulating Properties: Motherwort contains flavonoids and alkaloids that may help regulate hormone levels, reducing the severity of menopausal symptoms like mood swings, anxiety, and depression.
Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: The decoction's ability to calm the nervous system can provide relief from anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, common complaints during menopause.
remedy_parts: Roots: Motherwort roots have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause.
Barks: The barks of the motherwort plant contain compounds that help regulate hormonal balance and reduce symptoms of menopause such as mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20-30 grams of dried leonurus cardiaca leaves and flowers for every liter of water.
Combine the herb material with 1 liter of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Store the filtered decoction in the refrigerator and use within 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to menopause that are treated with herbal decoctions are conditions associated with hormonal imbalance, symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and vaginal dryness. Herbs like Dong quai, Rehmannia, and Angelica are used to alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS), polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), and perimenopause. These herbal decoctions can help regulate hormones, reduce inflammation, and promote overall female reproductive health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with menopause is a blend of Black Cohosh, Red Clover, and Ginseng. Black Cohosh helps alleviate hot flashes and night sweats, while Red Clover supports hormonal balance and bone health. Ginseng, particularly American Ginseng, reduces stress and anxiety, promoting a restful sleep. This synergistic blend can also help alleviate mood swings, fatigue, and vaginal dryness, making it an effective natural solution for menopause symptoms. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Electronic physician, herbal decoctions are good for menopause because they can provide relief from common symptoms such as hot flashes. The study examined 20 medicinal plants used in clinical trials for the treatment of menopausal symptoms between 1994 and 2016, and found that plants like Sage herb (Salvia officinalis), Black cohosh (Cimicifuga racemosa), and Fenugreek (Trigonella foenum-graecum) were effective in treating acute menopausal syndrome with different mechanisms. By incorporating these herbal decoctions into their treatment plans, women can potentially alleviate menopausal symptoms and improve their overall health and well-being.
